evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implement data quality tests and monitoring for accuracy
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The responsibilities emphasize trusted datasets, data quality tests, monitoring, and documentation frameworks, with rigour signals clearly outnumbering any velocity language.
evidence.domain_risk: responsible for setting roadmaps and shipping products that impact financial results
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The squad's outputs are explicitly tied to financial results at a major retailer, but the language is business-impact framing rather than formal financial-reporting or regulatory compliance, placing it at moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: The Analytics Engineering squad operates as a "greenfield" team focused on a specific business domain with senior leadership support
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The squad is explicitly described as greenfield within a large established company, with named tools already in responsibilities, indicating a growing sub-team rather than a fully early-stage or fully mature function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Develop and maintain dbt models transforming raw data into "trusted datasets for analytics"
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical practices (CTEs, window functions, dimensional modeling, dbt certifications) show technical specificity only a hiring manager who has done the job would include.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support self-service analytics with reusable, high-quality data models
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role serves both the internal data function (self-service analytics, data engineers) and broader business/financial outcomes with genuinely split emphasis, making this mixed.
evidence.autonomy_level: responsible for setting roadmaps and shipping products that impact financial results
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The squad sets its own roadmap (strategic) while also translating specific business requirements and supporting data engineers on new source requirements (execution), reflecting genuine mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Although the company description mentions an AI and automation shift, the responsibilities section contains no AI-specific expectation of the candidate, so this defaults to none.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement data quality tests and monitoring for accuracy
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality tests and monitoring are framed as an owned responsibility using an explicit implementation verb, making this a responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Implement data quality tests and monitoring for accuracy
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality tests, monitoring, and trusted datasets are named responsibilities reflecting operational reliability concern, secondary to the primary roadmap-and-shipping mandate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: The Analytics Engineering squad operates as a "greenfield" team focused on a specific business domain
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The JD explicitly labels the squad and its mandate as greenfield.
evidence.loss_aversion: Implement data quality tests and monitoring for accuracy — reflects operational concern about data trust rather than formal compliance.
